classObjective: Apply the compound-interest formula to investment and depreciation problems.
workedExamples: 8-3, Example 3 — Model Real-World Growth and Decay
8-3, Example 4 — Apply Compound Interest Formula (A = P(1 + r/n)^nt)
8-3, Example 6 — Apply Compound Interest and Exponential Models (Half-Life)
warmUp: If $1000 is invested at 5% annual interest, compounded quarterly, how much is in the account after 1 year?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: A = P(1 + r/n)^nt; continuous growth formula; half-life models; investment and depreciation contexts
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice applying compound interest formula to investment and depreciation problems
independentPractice: Practice problems applying exponential formulas to compound interest, depreciation, and half-life scenarios
assessment: Exit ticket: how long does it take for an investment to double at 6% interest compounded continuously?
capReflection: How does compounding frequency affect the effective rate of return on an investment?
